meta.title: SafePassage — ቅድሚ ምስጋር ፍለጥ
meta.description: ናይ ዘይሕጋዊ ምስጋር ሞት ሓቀኛ ዳታን ዘሎ ሕጋዊ መንገድታትን።

hero.sub: ኣሽሓት ሰባት ኣብ ነፍሲ ወከፍ ዓመት ናብ ኤውሮጳ ብዘይሕጋዊ መንገዲ ክሰግሩ እናፈተኑ ይሞቱ። ክትፈልጥ ዘለካ/ኪ ኣንብብ — ድሮ ዘሎ ዝሓሸ ውሑስ መንገድታትን።

strip.text2: ኣብ ናብ ኤውሮጳ ናይ ስደት መንገድታት መዝጊቡ — ንዓሰርተ ዓመታት ኣብ ነፍሲ ወከፍ 5 ሰዓታት ሓደ ሞት።

dangers.label: ሓቂ
dangers.lead: ዝርዝር ቁጽርታት ካብ IOM፡ UNHCR፡ን ናይ ኤውሮጳ ዶብ ትካላት ዝተወሰዱ እዮም። ትርጉም ዝሰዓሩ ኣይኮኑን — ካብ ሓቂ ዝሓነፁ እዮም።

stats.label: ካብ 2014 ጀሚሩ ኣብ ናይ ኤውሮጳ ናይ ስደት መንገድታት ዝተመዝገቡ ሞት
stats.source: ናይ IOM ጠፊኦም ዘለዉ ስደተኛታት ፕሮጀክት, 2025

stats.label: ናይ ማእከላዊ ሜዲተረኒያን ምስጋር ብሞት ወይ ምስኣን ይዛዘም
ንሽርሕቲ ዝኽፈል — ናይ ድሕነት ወይ ምብጻሕ ዋስትና የለን

routes.label: ዝተዶኩሙ መንገድታት
routes.lead: ሽርሕቲ ቀሊል ምዃኑ ይነግሩኻ/ኺ። ዳታ ግን ካልእ ዕላል ይነግር።

items.name: ማእከላዊ ሜዲተረኒያን
items.badge: ኣብ ዓለም ዝዛረ ናይ ባሕሪ መንገዲ
items.desc: ሊቢያ/ቱኒዝያ → ጣልያን/ማልጣ። ን ባሕሪ ዘይኮናስ ንወሓዚ ዝተሰርሑ ናይ ጎማ ጃልባታት። ምሉኣት ሰባት። ምቁራዕ ልሙድ እዩ።

items.name: ኣትላንቲክ — ካናሪ ደሴታት
items.desc: ምዕራብ ኣፍሪቃ → እስፓኛ። ዝቐልጠፈ ዕቤትን ኣብ ነፍሲ ወከፍ ምብጋስ ዝዛረ መንገዲ። ብዙሕ ጃልባታት ፈጺሙ ኣይረኽባን።

items.name: ናይ ባልካን ብምድሪ መንገዲ
items.desc: ቱርኪ → ግሪኽ → ሰርብያ → ሃንጋሪ። ሰባት ኣብ ዘዝቀዘቀ ሙቐት ብኣኽራን ምሊኦም ሚእቲ ኪሎሜተራት ይኸዱ።

items.name: ሳሃራ በረኻ
items.desc: ደቡብ ሳሃራ ኣፍሪቃ → ሰሜን ኣፍሪቃ። ሳሃራ ካብ ባሕሪ ዝዛረ ስደተኛታት ይቐትሎም — ግን ሬሳታት ብዘይሕብሩ ኣይርከቡን።

items.name: ናይ እንግሊዝ ቻናል
items.badge: ካብ ዝተሓታቱ ናይ ዓለም ናይ ምልላፍ ሽፋናት
items.desc: ፈረንሳ → ዩናይትድ ኪንግደም። ንኣሽቱ ጃልባታት ካብ ናይ መርከብ ማዕበል ክቕለቡ ይኽእላ። ካብ 2018 ዝበዝሑ 50 ሰባት ሞይቶም።

items.name: ኣብ ማካይን ምኽዋር
items.desc: ናይ ናይ ጽዕነት ዕቃቦ፡ ናይ ምዝሃር ማካይን። ሰባት ብሙቐት፡ ቁሩር፡ ወይ ካርቦን ሞኖክሳይድ ይሞቱ። ኣብ 2019፡ 39 ቬትናማውያን ኣብ ኤሴክስ ዩኬ ኣብ ጽዕነት ማኪና ሞይቶም ተረኽቡ።

legal.label: ዝሓሸ ውሑስ መንገድታት ኣሎ
legal.lead: ክልተ ሕቶታት መልስ ሃብ ንሕና ድማ ንኩነታትካ/ኺ ዝምጥን ሕጋዊ መንገድ ንሕብረካ/ኺ።

paths.name: ናይ ዑቕባ ሕቶ
paths.desc: ካብ ምስጓም፡ ኲናት፡ ወይ ዓመጽ እናሃደምካ/ኺ እንተኾይንካ/ኺ፡ ኣብ ትሕቲ ናይ 1951 ዕቑባ ስምምዕ ናይ ስደተኛ ደረጃ ኣሎካ/ኪ ክኸውን ይኽእል።
paths.how: ናብ ቀረባ ቤት ጽሕፈት UNHCR ወይ ሃገራዊ ናይ ዑቕባ ስልጣን ኪድ። ዝኾነ ሰነዳት ኣምጽእ — ሰነዳት ምስኣን ኣዩካ/ኪ ኣይቅጽዕን።

paths.name: ናይ UNHCR ዳግም ምቕማጥ
paths.desc: UNHCR ዝበዝሕ ምስቅቃል ዘለዎም ዝተዘርጉ ስደተኛታት ናብ ሳልሳይ ውሑስ ሃገር ንምቕማጥ ትመርጽ።
paths.how: ኣብ ቀረባ ቤት ጽሕፈት UNHCR ምዝገብ። ዳግም ምቕማጥ ን'ምቕሓፍ' ንዝኾነ ሰብ ኣይኸፍልን — ኩሉ ሳዕ ብናጻ እዩ።

paths.name: ናይ ክኢላ ሰራሕተኛ ቪዛ
paths.desc: ዩኬ፡ ጀርመን፡ ካናዳ፡ን ካልኦት ሃገራት ኣብ ጥዕና፡ ቴክኖሎጂ፡ ህንጻ፡ን ሞያ ዘሎ ክኢላ ሰራሕተኛ ይደልያ።
paths.how: ናይ ዩኬ ናይ ኣጻፋፍ ሞያ ዝርዝር ወይ 'Make it in Germany' ፖርታል ርአ። ኣብ ቤት ኤምባሲ ወይ ቆንሱሊ ሕቶ ቅረብ።

paths.name: ናይ ተምሃሮ ቪዛ
paths.desc: ናብ ዩኒቨርሲቲ ወይ ኮሌጅ ተቐቢልካ/ኺ እንተኾይንካ/ኺ፡ ብናይ ተምሃሮ ቪዛ ብሕጋዊ ክትኣቱ ትኽእል/ኢ። ብዙሕ ሃገራት ናይ ርብዒ ፍርቂ ሰዓት ስራሕ ይፈቅዳ።
paths.how: ቅድሚ ኹሉ ናይ ቡርሳ ሕቶ ቅረብ (Chevening, DAAD, Erasmus). ናይ ቅቡልነት ደብዳቤ ካብ ትካል ቀንዲ ረቂቕ እዩ።

paths.name: ናይ ስድራቤት ምትእኽኻብ
paths.desc: ቀረባ ኣባል ስድራቤት (ሰብ ሓዳር፡ ወላዲ፡ ቈልዓ) ኣብ ናይ ኤውሮጳ ሃገር ወይ ዩኬ ሕጋዊ ደረጃ ዘለዎ/ዎ እንተኾይኑ/ና፡ ብሕጋዊ ናብኡ/ናቦ ክትኸይድ ትኽእል/ኢ።
paths.how: ኣብ ኤውሮጳ ዘሎ ስድራቤትካ/ኺ ኣብ ስምካ/ኪ ሕቶ ይቕርብ። ኩነታት ብሃገር ይፈላለ።

paths.name: ሰብኣዊ ቪዛ
paths.desc: ዝተወሰና ሃገራት ኣብ ቤት ኤምባሲ ሰብኣዊ ቪዛ ይህባ፡ ምብጻሕ ከም ዋሕስ ዑቕባ ዝሕቶ ሕጋዊ ምእታው ፈቒዱ — ዝኾነ ሓደጋ ዝመልኦ ምስጋር ብዘይምፍጻም።
paths.how: ናብ ናይ ኤውሮጳ ቤት ኤምባሲ ወይ ቆንሱሊ ኣብ ሃገርካ/ኺ ተወከስ። ኩነታትካ/ኺ ብሩህ ኩን/ኢ። ፖርቱጋል፡ እስፓኛ፡ ን ጣልያን ነዚ ኣካይዳ ተጠቒሞም ኣለዉ።

rows.illegal: ኣዝዩ ልዑል — ኣብ ዝተወሰና መንገድታት ካብ 45 1 ይሞቱ
rows.legal: ትሑት — ብነፋሪት፡ ኣውቶቡስ፡ ወይ ባቡር ትጓዓዝ
UNHCR ብናጻ ዩ። ናይ ቪዛ ክፍሊት ካብ ዋጋ ሽርሕቲ ኣዝዩ ውሑድ ዩ።

rows.illegal: ዘይተዘርጉ — ናይ ቅጽበታዊ ማእሰርቲ ን ምስጓምን ሓደጋ
rows.legal: ምሉእ ሕጋዊ መሰላት፡ ናብ ኣገልጎሎት ምርካብ፡ ናይ ስራሕ ፍቓዳት

rows.illegal: ዘይሕጋዊ ምእታው ናይ መጻኢ ናይ ቪዛ ን ዑቕባ ሕቶታት ይዓፍኖ
rows.legal: ናይ ገበን ሰነድ የለን — ናይ መጻኢ ኣማራጺታትካ/ኺ ክፉት ይተርፍ

rows.illegal: ስድራቤትካ/ኺ እንታይ ከም ዝፈጸሞ ፈጺሞም ክፈልጡ ይኽእሉ ኣይኮኑን
rows.legal: ብድሓር ናይ ስድራቤት ምትእኽኻብ ብሕጋዊ ስድራቤትካ/ኺ ምጻእ ትኽእል/ኢ

rows.illegal: ዋላ ሓደ — ካብ ኩሎም ኣገልጎሎት ተሓቢእካ/ኺ ኣለኻ/ኺ
rows.legal: ሕጋዊ ናይ ዑቕባ ሕቶ ቅናሕ ኣቕሊ ን ሕክምናዊ ክንክን ይረክቡ

items.quote: ሽርሕቲ ጃልባ ውሑስ ምዃኑ ኣፈለጠ። 80 ሰብ ኣብ ን20 ሰብ ዝተሰርሐ ጎማ ጃልባ ነይርና። ሓወይ ድሒኑ ኣይኮነን። ምለስ ክብል ዝዓዲ ዘሎ ኩሉ ኔሩ ካሊእ ምርጫ ምገበርኩ ኔሩ።
items.name: ዝደሓነ — ማእከላዊ ሜዲተረኒያን፡ 2022

items.quote: €8,000 ከፊልና ኣብ ጥሪ ኣብ ናይ ሰርብያ ኣኽራን ጠፊዕና። ሰለስተ ሰባት ካብ ጓናይ ብቁሩር ሞይቶም። 11 መዓልቲ ተጓዒዝና።
items.name: ዝደሓነ — ናይ ባልካን መንገዲ፡ 2021

items.quote: ኣብ ፓኪስታን ኣብ UNHCR ምዝጊቤ። 14 ወርሒ ወሲዱ ግን ብሕጋዊ ናብ ጀርመን ዳግም ምቕማጥ ተጠቒሚ። ሕጂ ስራሕ ኣሎኒ። ደቀይ ናብ ቤት ትምህርቲ ይኸዱ። በጃኻ/ኺ — ንሽርሕቲ ኣይትእመኑ።
items.name: ዳግም ዝቐመጠ ስደተኛ — ጀርመን፡ 2023

cta.label: ውሑስ መንገዲ ወስድ
cta.title: ቅድሚ ምውሳን ሕጋዊ መንገድኻ/ኺ ፍለጥ
cta.sub: ቅድሚ ንሽርሕቲ ዝኾነ ገንዘብ ምኽፋልካ/ኺ UNHCR ወይ ናይ ሕጋዊ ሓገዝ ትካል ተወከስ። እዚ ሓበሬታ ብናጻ ዩ።

footer.disclaimer: እዚ ፕላትፎርም ካብ IOM፡ UNHCR፡ Frontex፡ ን Europol ናይ ህዝቢ ዳታ ይጥቀም። ናይ ሓበሬታ ምንጪ ጥራይ ዩ ናይ ሕጋዊ ምኽሪ ኣይኮነን።
footer.sources: ዳታ፡ IOM ጠፊኦም ዘለዉ ስደተኛታት ፕሮጀክት · ናይ UNHCR ዓለምለኻዊ ኩነታት · ናይ Frontex ናይ ሓደጋ ዳዕምዳዕ 2025 · ናይ Europol ጸብጻብ 2024

thinkCarefully.label: Before You Decide
thinkCarefully.lead: Migration can save lives — but it can also destroy them. If you have safety, family, and basic stability at home, the reality of irregular migration is rarely worth it. Read this honestly before you decide.

cards.title: Do you have housing and food security?
cards.body: If you have a home, food, and your family is with you — you already have more than millions of people in the world. Migration to the UK or EU does not guarantee any of these things. Many arrivals spend years in asylum hostels, shared housing, or temporary accommodation.

cards.title: Are you leaving economic frustration — not danger?
cards.body: If you are not facing persecution or direct physical danger, the legal economic migration routes require qualifications, job offers, and language skills. Irregular migration for economic reasons almost always leads to exploitation, not prosperity. The UK minimum wage sounds good — but rent in London starts at £1,400/month for a shared room.

cards.title: What will this cost your family?
cards.body: The smugglers take your family's savings — sometimes everything they own. If you die or disappear at sea, they lose both the money and you. Families have sold land, borrowed from moneylenders at crushing rates, and been left in debt for years by a loved one who didn't survive the crossing.

cards.title: Could you build something at home?
cards.body: Many countries that send migrants to Europe are growing fast — Nigeria, Bangladesh, Kenya, Ghana, Egypt, Ethiopia. Professionals with international education or skills are in high demand. The person who builds a business at home may end up wealthier — and happier — than the one who cleans offices in a foreign city they don't belong to.

thinkCarefully.warning: If you are not in danger — think very carefully. The Europe many people imagine does not exist. The Europe that does exist is cold, expensive, and often lonely for those who arrive without legal status, language skills, or qualifications.

crudeReality.label: The Reality Nobody Tells You
crudeReality.lead: Smugglers and social media show you the dream. Nobody shows you what comes after — even for those who arrive legally.

items.title: The housing crisis is real
items.body: UK and EU cities are facing severe housing shortages. A single room in a shared house in London costs £900–£1,400/month. Many newly arrived migrants — including those with work visas — sleep in garden sheds, overcrowded HMOs, or on friends' floors for the first years. Homelessness among migrants is rising, not falling.

items.title: Your qualifications may not be recognised
items.body: A doctor, engineer, or teacher from Nigeria, Bangladesh, or Pakistan often cannot practise in the UK without re-qualifying. This can take 3–7 years and cost tens of thousands of pounds. Many highly educated migrants end up working in warehouses, delivery, or care homes while paying off debts from their journey.

items.title: The mental health toll is severe
items.body: Isolation, language barriers, discrimination, and the gap between expectation and reality cause serious mental health crises among migrants. NHS mental health services have years-long waiting lists. Suicide rates among asylum seekers and undocumented migrants are significantly higher than the general population.

items.title: The cost of living is brutal
items.body: A grocery shop for one week in the UK costs £50–£80. Electricity, water, council tax, transport, and phone add another £400–£600/month. A minimum wage job (£11.44/hour) earns roughly £1,800/month after tax. After rent, there is almost nothing left — and nothing to send home.

items.title: Undocumented life is not 'freedom'
items.body: Without documentation you cannot open a bank account, rent legally, access healthcare fully, or leave and re-enter the country. You live in permanent fear of immigration enforcement. Many people live this way for 10–20 years, unable to visit family when parents die, unable to move forward, unable to go back.

items.title: Exploitation does not end at the border
items.body: Labour trafficking, sexual exploitation, and debt bondage continue inside Europe. Undocumented migrants are targeted because they cannot report abuse to police without risking deportation. The people who exploit migrants at sea often have networks that exploit them on land too.

afterArrival.label: After Arrival — The Untold Stories
afterArrival.lead: Even people who arrive legally — with visas, qualifications, and ambition — face a reality that is far harder than the dream. These are documented experiences, not exceptions.

items.title: Graduated. Unemployed. Invisible.
items.body: Nigerian international students graduate from UK universities with first-class degrees — and send hundreds of CVs without a callback. Studies show that CVs with African names receive 60% fewer interview callbacks than identical CVs with English names. Discrimination in hiring is well-documented and ongoing.

items.title: Arrived legally. Living in a garage.
items.body: Hundreds of Bangladeshi workers on UK care visas have been stranded after employers collapsed, leaving them without work, housing, or income. Some lived in employer-provided accommodation that was a converted garage. The 60-day job-switch window was not enough to find new sponsors.

items.title: 10 years. Still waiting.
items.body: Many Somali refugees who arrived legally in the early 2010s are still in temporary housing, still waiting for PTSD treatment, still navigating a benefits system that treats them as burdens. Children born in the UK speak fluent English but their parents remain isolated, unable to integrate.

items.title: Skilled Worker. Refused at the airport.
items.body: Pakistani workers with valid UK Skilled Worker visas have been turned away at the border after being unable to answer detailed questions about their employer or role. Visa documents were genuine — but insufficient preparation for the border interview led to refusal of leave to enter.

comeHome.label: A Message for Students
comeHome.title: Your country needs you back
comeHome.sub: If you came to study — go home. Not because Europe is not worth experiencing, but because you are worth more to your country than to a delivery company in Manchester.

points.text: Nigeria, Bangladesh, Kenya, Ghana, and Ethiopia are building fast. Engineers, doctors, teachers, and entrepreneurs who return with international experience are transforming these countries from the inside.
Your purchasing power at home is dramatically higher. A salary that feels small in the UK can fund a house, a business, and a family's security in many home countries.

Your parents are ageing. Your language, your food, your community — these things matter more than a passport stamp. Many people who stay in Europe for decades describe a deep and lasting loneliness that money never resolves.
The most powerful thing you can do for your country is return with skills and build something. You cannot change your home country from a shared house in Birmingham.

comeHome.cta: Use the legal routes to study, gain experience, then return. That is the migration story that actually changes lives — yours and your country's.
